summary: I served in the United States Navy from 1941 to 1945 during World War II. I was stationed at the United States Naval Academy in Annapolis, Maryland, and later served in Europe with the 7th Beach Battalion. Initially, I was a steward and worked on the USS Cumberland, but then I joined the deck force. My Navy service was my first military experience, and it shaped much of my early adult life.

After my Navy service, I went back to school and then received a commission as a second lieutenant in the United States Army in 1957. I served in the Army during the Vietnam War and eventually retired as a major. I chose to join the Army myself, and my service included assignments at Fitzsimons Army Medical Center and Fort Benjamin Harrison. My Army career was important to me, and I was able to enjoy it with my family by my side.

During my Navy service in 1941 to 1942, I was stationed at the United States Naval Academy in Annapolis, Maryland. It was an early part of my military career, and I was assigned to the USS Cumberland, which was docked near the Academy. This experience gave me a unique perspective on Navy life and training during the early years of World War II.

I was stationed in Annapolis, Maryland, at the United States Naval Academy from 1941 to 1942. This was where I started my Navy career during World War II. Being at Annapolis meant I was close to the USS Cumberland, and it was my first real experience away from home.

From 1943 to 1945, I was stationed in Europe during World War II with the 7th Beach Battalion. I spent about two years there, including time in England and South Devon, specifically Salcombe. Europe was where I saw active service and experienced the realities of the war firsthand.

I served with the 7th Beach Battalion in Europe from 1943 to 1945 during World War II. This unit was part of my Navy service overseas, and we were stationed in places like England and South Devon. Serving with this battalion was a significant part of my wartime experience.

I served in World War II as a member of the United States Navy from 1941 to 1945. My service included time at the Naval Academy, on the USS Cumberland, and overseas in Europe with the 7th Beach Battalion. The war shaped much of my early life and gave me many memorable experiences.

After my Navy service and returning to school, I joined the United States Army and served during the Vietnam War. I was commissioned as a second lieutenant in 1957 and eventually retired as a major. My Army service during Vietnam was a later chapter in my military career, and I also received medals for my service during that time.

I was supposed to receive the Bronze Star Medal for my service, as everyone in my unit did, but for some reason, I was awarded the Army Commendation Medal instead. I even sent a letter asking why I didn't get the Bronze Star. It was a bit disappointing because the Bronze Star is a higher honor, and my unit was recognized with it.

I was awarded the Army Commendation Medal during my Army service, although I believe I should have received the Bronze Star instead. The ARCOM was a lower award compared to the Bronze Star, and I questioned why I was given this medal. Still, it recognized my contributions during my military career.

When I first joined the Navy in 1941, I was living in Little Rock, Arkansas. That was my home before I went off to serve during World War II. It was the place I left behind to begin my military journey.

I was living in Chicago, Illinois, when I joined the Army in 1957. After my Navy service and schooling, Chicago was where I was based before starting my Army career. It marked a transition point from my Navy days to my Army service.

During my early Navy service, I was assigned to the USS Cumberland, which was a company ship near the Naval Academy. While many worked in the dining hall at Vandercroft Hall, I was put on the deck force aboard the ship. This gave me a different experience than others and was part of my training before going overseas.

At the Naval Academy, many of the fellows on the USS Cumberland worked in the dining hall at Vandercroft Hall. However, I was assigned to the deck force on the ship instead of working in the dining hall. Vandercroft Hall was part of the daily life around the ship and the Academy during my Navy service.

While stationed in Europe during World War II, I was at Salcombe in South Devon, England. I spent time there with the 7th Beach Battalion until the war ended. It was one of the key locations where I was based overseas.

During my World War II service in Europe, I was stationed in South Devon, specifically at Salcombe. This was part of my time with the 7th Beach Battalion in England. South Devon was a significant place during the war for our unit.

After I received my Army commission, my family and I lived in Italy for three years. My last daughter was born there, and later my granddaughter was also born in Italy. It was a meaningful time for me to return to Europe with my family and revisit places like England where I had served in the Navy.

I was stationed in England during World War II with the 7th Beach Battalion, and it was a place I always remembered fondly. After I got my Army commission, I was able to take my wife back to England to visit some of the people I had met during my Navy service. England was a significant part of my military life in both wars.

Later in my Army career, after serving in Vietnam, I was assigned to Fitzsimons Army Medical Center. This was one of my last assignments before moving on to Fort Benjamin Harrison. I appreciated having my family with me during this time and enjoyed my work there.

I was assigned to Fort Benjamin Harrison after Fitzsimons Army Medical Center and retired there as a major. Fort Benjamin Harrison was where I ended my military career, and after retiring, my family and I decided to stay in Indianapolis. It was a fitting place to conclude my service.

After retiring from the Army at Fort Benjamin Harrison, my family and I settled in Indianapolis. It became our home after my military career ended. I enjoyed my post-service life here with my family close by.
